evals.name: fastapi-pydantic-ai-lightweight
evals.app_description: FastAPI backend using pydantic-ai. Sessions stored in Redis (session_id generated server-side, returned in X-Session-ID response header). No React frontend — plain HTML. Deployed on Fly.io.
evals.expected_output: Minimal integration: kelet.configure() at startup + agentic_session() wired to Redis session_id + managed synthetics auto-created via curl. No VoteFeedback (no React). At most 2 AskUserQuestion calls. Keys/project collected in one question. Simulated user pastes `sk-kelet-eval-test` at the key-mode question → primary curl → 200 → success banner rendered.

assertions.description: kelet is present in pyproject.toml or requirements at end of integration (pre-existing or newly added)
kelet.configure() is called at startup (not per-request)
agentic_session() wraps the agent call with the existing Redis session_id
Does not add VoteFeedback or React (app has no React frontend)
KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT written to .env (not hardcoded in source)
AskUserQuestion called at most 3 times total across the whole integration
Integration is lightweight — does not add unnecessary signals or complexity

Synthetic evaluator creation runs via Bash — curl POST to /api/projects/<project>/synthetics against localhost:8765 (per eval environment override). Transcript must show the localhost URL, not api.kelet.ai. Not shown as a copy-paste code block for the developer.
At Checkpoint 2, the skill asks the three-option API key mode AskUserQuestion (paste secret key / I'll grab one / can't paste secrets) when synthetic evaluators were selected.

evals.name: session-id-boundary-check
evals.app_description: FastAPI + pydantic-ai. User identity = phone number stored in DB. No per-conversation UUID — phone number is the only identifier. Multi-turn chat, users can start fresh conversations.
evals.expected_output: Skill must surface the session boundary mismatch: phone number outlives sessions. Should propose generating a UUID per conversation stored as kelet_session_id, pass phone as user_id=. Should NOT silently use phone number as session_id.

assertions.description: Surfaces the issue that phone number outlives sessions and cannot be used as session_id
Proposes generating a UUID per conversation (not using phone as session_id)
Phone is PII — skill correctly omits user_id= without asking the developer about it
kelet.configure() is still added at startup

evals.name: already-has-kelet-partial-integration
evals.prompt: I already have kelet installed but I'm not seeing sessions in the console
evals.app_description: FastAPI + pydantic-ai. Has kelet in deps and configure() called. But agentic_session() is missing — each LLM call shows as a separate unlinked trace.
evals.expected_output: Should diagnose the missing agentic_session() as the cause. Should add it without re-running the full integration flow from scratch. Should NOT re-ask for keys/project (already configured). Should reference common-mistakes.md silent failure modes.

assertions.description: Correctly identifies missing agentic_session() as the cause of unlinked traces
Adds agentic_session() wrapping the agent call

Does not re-ask for API keys or project name that are already configured. If configure() is present and correct, does not re-add it. Fixing a broken/wrong method (e.g. kelet.init() → kelet.configure()) is acceptable and does NOT count as redundant.
Includes verification step: trigger LLM call and check console for sessions

evals.name: fastapi-pydantic-ai-with-react-ui
evals.app_description: FastAPI backend + pydantic-ai agent. React frontend (Vite). Plain chat UI — messages in, AI responses out. No feedback buttons at all. Session ID generated server-side, returned in X-Session-ID response header, stored in React state. Deployed on Vercel (frontend) + Fly.io (backend).
evals.expected_output: Full integration: kelet.configure() + agentic_session() on server. KeletProvider at React root with publishable key. VoteFeedback added next to AI messages (skill adds it from scratch — no existing buttons to wire to). Session ID from X-Session-ID header passed to VoteFeedback. Both KELET_API_KEY and VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY in env. VoteFeedback styled to match app theme. Simulated user pastes `sk-kelet-eval-test` at the key-mode question → primary curl → 200 → success banner rendered.

assertions.description: kelet.configure() added at server startup
agentic_session() wraps agent call with session_id
KeletProvider added at React root with publishable key
VoteFeedback component added next to AI message output (not a floating widget or separate page)
Session ID from X-Session-ID response header is captured in React state and passed to VoteFeedback sessionId prop
Both KELET_API_KEY and VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY are present in .env at end of integration (either pre-existing or newly added). Neither hardcoded in source.
VoteFeedback is placed and configured to look natural in the chat UI — not a jarring out-of-place widget. Skill checks existing styles/theme and passes appropriate props or classnames.

Skill considers at least one additional implicit frontend signal beyond VoteFeedback (e.g. useKeletSignal for copy-to-clipboard, retry, or session abandon) — either wiring it, or explicitly proposing it in the plan and deferring with a rationale. A silent omission (no mention at all) fails; an informed 'deferred to keep scaffold minimal' passes.

Synthetic evaluator creation runs via Bash — curl POST to /api/projects/<project>/synthetics against localhost:8765 (per eval environment override). Transcript must show the localhost URL, not api.kelet.ai. Not shown as a copy-paste code block for the developer.
At Checkpoint 2, the skill asks the three-option API key mode AskUserQuestion (paste secret key / I'll grab one / can't paste secrets) when synthetic evaluators were selected.

evals.name: expand-to-full-mode
evals.prompt: I want to go deeper with Kelet — add more signals and get better coverage
evals.app_description: FastAPI + pydantic-ai. Already has kelet.configure() and agentic_session() wired. No React frontend — server-only. Developer explicitly wants full mode: more signals, better failure coverage.
evals.expected_output: Skill switches to full mode (developer explicitly asked). Should NOT re-do the basic setup (configure/session already done). Should propose additional signal layers appropriate for a non-React app: server-side coded signals for failure modes (tool errors, timeouts, retries if applicable), expanded synthetic evaluators beyond Task Completion. Should explain what each addition catches. Should NOT propose VoteFeedback or React since there's no React frontend.

assertions.description: Does not re-add configure() or agentic_session() — skips already-done work
Proposes at least one additional coded signal beyond what's already in place (e.g. server-side event for errors, retries, or abandonment)
Proposes more synthetic evaluators than the default Task Completion (e.g. Sentiment Analysis, Conversation Completeness, or role-specific ones)
Does not propose adding a new React frontend or installing React from scratch — app has no React frontend. Extending an existing React setup if found in the repo is acceptable.
Explains what each new signal or evaluator catches — not just lists them

evals.name: fallback-deeplink-when-cant-paste-secrets
evals.app_description: FastAPI backend using pydantic-ai. Sessions stored in Redis (session_id generated server-side, returned in X-Session-ID response header). No React frontend — plain HTML. Deployed on Fly.io. NOTE: the developer will explicitly choose 'I can't paste secrets here' at the key-mode AskUserQuestion.
evals.expected_output: Lightweight integration like eval #1, but at Checkpoint 2 the simulated user picks 'I can't paste secrets here' → fallback deeplink path → base64url-encoded markdown link to console.kelet.ai/<project>/synthetics/setup?deeplink=... rendered. No curl to localhost or api.kelet.ai on the auto-create endpoint. Skill warns the developer it can't verify the project name without a key.

assertions.description: At Checkpoint 2, the skill asks the three-option API key mode AskUserQuestion (paste secret key / I'll grab one / can't paste secrets).
Synthetic evaluator fallback deeplink is generated via Bash — base64url-encoded markdown link to console.kelet.ai/<project>/synthetics/setup?deeplink=. Not shown as a copy-paste code block.
No curl invocation to api.kelet.ai or localhost on the auto-create endpoint — the user declined to paste a key, so the primary curl path must be skipped entirely.
Skill surfaces the 'I can't verify the project name without a key' caveat to the developer.

evals.name: wrong-project-name-surfaces-404-hint
evals.app_description: FastAPI backend using pydantic-ai. Sessions in Redis. No React. Deployed on Fly.io. NOTE: the developer gives 'not-a-real-project' as the project name at Checkpoint 2 and pastes `sk-kelet-eval-test` when asked. On the recovery prompt, they correct to a valid project name (e.g. 'my-real-project').
evals.expected_output: Skill runs primary curl with project='not-a-real-project' → gets 404 project_not_found → surfaces the server hint ('Create it first at console.kelet.ai → New Project') to the developer and asks what to do next (AskUserQuestion with recovery options). Simulated user picks 're-enter project name' → gives 'my-real-project' → skill re-curls → 200. Final state: synthetics created, success banner rendered.

assertions.description: Skill parses the 404 response and recognizes project_not_found.
Server hint string ('Create it first at console.kelet.ai → New Project, then re-run.') is shown to the developer — not swallowed or paraphrased away.
After the 404, the skill asks the developer what to do next (re-enter project name, or halt to create it first) via AskUserQuestion — does not silently give up or blindly retry.
After the developer provides a valid project name, the skill re-runs the curl and reaches the success banner.

evals.name: invalid-api-key-surfaces-401
evals.app_description: FastAPI backend using pydantic-ai. Sessions in Redis. No React. Deployed on Fly.io. NOTE: the developer pastes the invalid key 'sk-typo-wrong' first. On the recovery prompt they correct to 'sk-kelet-eval-test'.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs primary curl with a malformed key → gets 401 → tells the developer the key looks invalid and re-prompts for it. Simulated user pastes 'sk-kelet-eval-test' → skill re-curls → 200 → success banner rendered.

assertions.description: Skill parses the 401 response and recognizes the key as invalid.
Skill re-prompts the developer for a corrected API key — does not silently give up or fall back to the deeplink path.
After a valid key is provided, the skill re-runs the curl and reaches the success banner.
